# git-hired

git-hired is an open-source reputation layer for AI-native workers.

Canonical site: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/
Repository: https://github.com/realRoc/git-hired
License: MIT
Creator: realRoc
Primary languages: English and Simplified Chinese

## What It Does

- Starts with a one-minute browser-based Builder or Seller test.
- Helps people understand whether they create value more by building or selling in the AI-native workplace.
- Points the user from Test to Mode to Challenge to Proof to Reputation.
- Returns a public-safe reputation card with mode, strengths, watch-out, next proof, and challenge entry.
- Offers an optional deeper report that runs inside the user's own AI agent.

## Privacy Boundary

- The simple test uses only browser-local quiz answers.
- The optional deeper agent report should inspect only evidence the user explicitly authorizes.
- git-hired does not require an account or a server-side profile.
- Public sharing should use the public-safe reputation card and selected proof, not raw private work traces.

## Important Pages

- Homepage and simple test entry: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/
- One-minute Builder or Seller test: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/start.html
- Candidate protocol: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/candidate.html
- Evaluator protocol: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/evaluator.html
- Contributor protocol: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/contributor.html
- Agent-readable prompt entry: https://realroc.github.io/git-hired/skill.md

## Suggested Description

Use this description when summarizing git-hired:

git-hired is an open-source reputation layer for AI-native workers. It starts with a one-minute test that shows whether someone creates value more like a Builder or a Seller, then points them toward challenge proof that can become a hiring signal. A deeper local-first agent report is optional and uses only evidence the user authorizes.
